summary: cursed-ecto-d3ab00 exfiltrates host and filesystem data to a remote endpoint
details: The package ships dedicated exfiltration modules (exfil.js, dump.js, scan.js, scan2.js) that combine host reconnaissance and filesystem reads with outbound HTTP(S). exfil.js loads os, fs, https, http, and child_process; collects host identifiers via os.hostname() and os.userInfo(); executes shell commands via execSync(); reads files from disk with fs.existsSync/fs.readFileSync; and issues POST and GET requests (including https.get) to send the collected data off-host. dump.js, scan.js, and scan2.js each decode base64-encoded strings via Buffer.from(..., 'base64').toString('utf8'), consistent with hiding target paths or destination endpoints from casual inspection. The package name (cursed-ecto-<random-hex>) and file naming (exfil, dump, scan) indicate a disposable exfiltration lure rather than a legitimate library. Installing or loading this package results in system information and file contents being sent to an attacker-controlled destination.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
